data.body:
## Context
After epics complete, project documentation drifts from reality — stale references, missing architecture changes, outdated commands. Today, the documentation update story created by /dso:preplanning is handled by a generic implementation agent with no specialized doc-writing logic, leading to inconsistent quality and doc bloat. The doc-writer agent applies a structured decision engine and 4-tier documentation schema to keep docs accurate and lean, serving both human readers (who need clear, task-based guides) and LLM agents (who need concise, declarative rules).

## Source Prompt Spec
doc-writer-agent.md (repo root) is the authoritative source prompt specification for the doc-writer agent. It contains detailed writing style rules per tier, specific Tier 3 file names, YAML frontmatter sync requirements, breakout notification format, and dual-audience style enforcement. This file MUST be referenced and carried through to stories, tasks, and implementation agents — the agent prompt authored during implementation should faithfully incorporate its full detail.

## Documentation Schema

| Tier | Name | Path | Content | Example |
|------|------|------|---------|---------|
| 1 | Navigation | Root (/) | Entry points for humans and agents | README.md, CLAUDE.md, llms.txt |
| 2 | User-Facing | /docs/user/ | Task-based how-to guides | How to configure X |
| 3 | Living Reference | /docs/reference/ | Current system state (destructive overwrites) | system-landscape.md, domain-logic.md |
| 4 | ADRs | /docs/adr/ | Immutable decision records | 0001-switch-to-redis.md |

## Decision Engine Gates (evaluated in order)

1. No-Op gate: If neither git diff nor story descriptions contain doc-relevant signals, emit structured no-op report and halt
2. User Impact gate: If the change affects a user-visible workflow, write or update Tier 2
3. Architectural gate: If the change affects a structural boundary, data flow, or introduces new technology, write or update Tier 3 and create a Tier 4 ADR
4. Constraint gate: If the change modifies a naming convention, tool command, or file location, update Tier 1 navigation files (excluding CLAUDE.md — see SC6)

Gates are evaluated in sequence; each gate that fires triggers its associated writes. The No-Op gate short-circuits only when no other gate would fire.

## Success Criteria

Phase A — Agent + Sprint Integration:
1. A dedicated dso:doc-writer agent exists in plugins/dso/agents/ and can be dispatched by /dso:sprint when processing a documentation update story (identified by title matching the pattern "Update project docs to reflect [epic summary]" as generated by /dso:preplanning)
2. The agent enforces the 4-tier documentation schema defined above
3. The agent evaluates the decision engine gates in the specified order before writing any documentation, and emits a structured no-op report (reason string, list of gates evaluated, pass/fail per gate) visible in the story completion output when no documentation changes are made
4. The agent reads both epic context (story/task descriptions + success criteria) and git diff to determine what changed
5. The agent applies the breakout heuristic: when a Tier 3 section would exceed ~1500 tokens or reach 3rd-level header nesting, the agent extracts the content into a new file under /docs/reference/subsystems/ and leaves a one-sentence summary with a link in the parent document
6. The agent treats Tier 1 navigation files that are also safeguard files (CLAUDE.md) as read-only and emits a suggested-change report rather than writing directly
7. When git diff exceeds the agent context capacity, the agent logs a truncation warning and flags affected decision-engine outputs as potentially incomplete

Phase B — Standalone Skill:
8. A /dso:update-docs skill exists (with SUB-AGENT-GUARD) that invokes the doc-writer agent for standalone use outside sprint context, accepting an optional commit range to scope the diff

Validation:
9. A test fixture exists with a known git diff and expected documentation output; the agent produces output that matches the expected tier placement (zero incorrectly placed files) and contains no hallucinated cross-references
10. The agent is run on the most recently completed epic as a dogfooding test. Its output is evaluated on three dimensions: (a) changed-section identification accuracy, (b) tier placement correctness, (c) absence of hallucinated references. The epic is not closed until all three dimensions pass and the comparison is recorded in plugins/dso/docs/designs/doc-writer-dogfood-result.md. Gaps are filed as follow-on bug tickets.

Cleanup:
11. doc-writer-agent.md is deleted from the repo root after all other tasks for the epic are complete

## Scenario Analysis
- Tier 1 destructive writes to CLAUDE.md violate the safeguard-files rule (critical): Addressed by SC6.
- Concurrent doc-writer dispatches from parallel story processing corrupt Tier 3 files (critical): Doc story depends on all implementation stories, so it runs last in the sprint batch.
- ADR sequential numbering collides under concurrent sprint sessions (critical): Two concurrent worktree sessions could both attempt to write the same ADR number.
- Git diff truncation silently omits architectural changes (high): Addressed by SC7.
- Sprint dispatcher passes wrong story type to doc-writer (high): Addressed by SC1.
- Agent file missing from agent-routing.conf (high): Addressed by Dependency 3.
- Sub-agent guard check not present (high): Addressed by SC8.
- 4-tier path schema hardcoded (high): Paths are hardcoded in v1; configurable paths are out of scope.
- Decision engine No-Op gate suppresses docs when context is minimal (medium): Agent uses both epic context AND git diff.
- Decision engine gates applied in wrong order (medium): Addressed by specifying evaluation order.

## Approach
Create a dedicated plugin agent at plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer/ following existing agent patterns (like dso:red-test-writer, dso:completion-verifier). Sprint dispatches it when encountering the documentation update story. A standalone /dso:update-docs skill provides ad-hoc invocation outside sprint context.

## Dependencies
1. /dso:sprint dispatch modification — the sprint skill story-routing logic must be updated to detect documentation update stories and dispatch dso:doc-writer. This touches a safeguarded skill file and requires user approval.
2. Canonical schema definition — the 4-tier schema and decision engine gates defined in this spec become the authoritative source; authoring them as part of the agent prompt is in-scope work.
3. Agent routing registration — dso:doc-writer must be added to agent-routing.conf.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — no bright-line conditions fired
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 10 scenarios surviving blue team filter (out of 15 red team)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
